title: 8 Top-Rated Herbal Creams for Eczema Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the skin, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing eczema symptoms.
Anthraquinones are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, which are common symptoms of eczema.
Flavonoids are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also contain antioxidants that protect the skin from damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a popular remedy for eczema because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The gel inside the aloe plant has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. When applied to the skin, aloe barbadensis cream creates a protective barrier that keeps moisture in and irritants out, making it easier to manage dry, itchy skin. The cream also contains vitamins A, C, and E, which help to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection. Additionally, aloe barbadensis cream is rich in antioxidants that fight off free radicals, which can further irritate eczema-prone skin. By using aloe barbadensis cream regularly, people with eczema can help to calm and comfort their skin, reducing the frequency and severity of outbreaks.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Plantago major cream for eczema, first, you'll need to harvest and dry the leaves of the Plantago major plant, also known as common plantain. You can find this plant in your garden or a nearby park, and make sure to identify it correctly. Dry the leaves thoroughly, either by air-drying them or using a dehydrator, to prevent any moisture from spoiling the plant. Next, blend the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the plant powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and a natural thickening agent, like beeswax, in a double boiler. Heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has reached a smooth consistency. Remove from heat and let it cool. Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator. This cream can be applied topically to help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eczema because it has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce redness and swelling, and promote healing.

Tannins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also act as a natural antiseptic to prevent bacterial infections that can make eczema worse.

Kaempferol is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage and promote healing.
Saponins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Flavonoids are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe itchy skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with eczema.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a helpful treatment for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce redness and swelling on the skin. The cream also has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help fight off any infections that can make eczema worse. Calendula officinalis contains a substance called triterpenoids, which can soothe and calm the skin. This can help ease the itching and discomfort that comes with eczema. When applied to the skin, the cream can create a protective barrier that locks in moisture and keeps the skin hydrated. This is especially important for people with eczema, as dry skin can make symptoms worse.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago major herbal cream that helps with eczema, you will first need to dry the Plantago major leaves. This can be done by tying the leaves in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can chop them into small pieces and mix them with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of 1 part dried Plantago major to 3 parts carrier oil. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, allowing the Plantago major to infuse its healing properties into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Finally, mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as beeswax and coconut oil, to create a thick and soothing cream that can be applied directly to areas of eczema.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, and their antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm the skin.

Flavonoids are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage and promote healing.
Tannins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, thereby providing relief from itchy and irritated skin.

Polyphenols are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is often used to help with eczema. It is made from the root of the comfrey plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The cream works by soothing and calming irritated skin. It contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling, making it easier for the skin to heal. The cream also helps to repair damaged skin by promoting collagen production and improving skin elasticity. This can lead to faster healing and less scarring. Some people also use it to relieve itching and discomfort caused by eczema. The cream is usually applied directly to the affected area and can be used in combination with other treatments, such as topical steroids, to help manage symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago major herbal creams that help with eczema, first you need to harvest the plant. Plantago major, also known as common plantain, grows in many parts of the world and has broad, oval-shaped leaves. Pick fresh leaves in the morning after the dew has dried, and wash them in cold water to clean them. Then, dry the leaves either by air-drying them or by placing them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees Fahrenheit for a few hours. Once the leaves are dry, you can make a plantain infusion by steeping a teaspoon of dried leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let the infusion cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Combine the cooled plantain infusion with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl until the beeswax or shea butter has melted, then pour the mixture into a clean jar and let it cool and thicken. Your homemade Plantago major cream is now ready to use, and can be applied directly to affected areas of the skin to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, making it a natural remedy to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Tannins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, and also act as an antioxidant to protect the skin from damage.

Isorhapontigenin has been found to be good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the swelling and redness associated with eczema, providing relief to people suffering from this skin condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica cream, also known as stinging nettle cream, is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms. The cream is made from the leaves of the stinging nettle plant, which contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When applied to the skin, the cream can reduce redness and swelling, and ease the itching and burning feelings associated with eczema. This is because the stinging nettle plant contains chemicals that help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that causes the skin to become inflamed and itchy. The cream also has moisturizing properties, which can help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further irritation. As a result, the cream can provide long-lasting relief from eczema symptoms, allowing the skin to heal and recover. The cream can be applied directly to the affected skin area, and can be used in combination with other treatments to help manage eczema symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago major herbal cream that helps with eczema, start by drying and grinding the leaves of the plant into a fine powder. You'll need about 2 tablespoons of dried Plantago major leaves for every 8 ounces of cream. Next, mix the powder with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to create a paste. Then, combine the paste with a cream base, such as shea butter or beeswax, and heat it gently in a double boiler until it's smooth and well combined. Once the mixture has cooled, add a few drops of essential oil like aloe vera or chamomile to give it a soothing scent and to enhance its healing properties. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean jar and store it in the fridge to keep it fresh and ready to use. When you need relief from eczema, simply apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area and gently massage it in.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness associated with eczema.
Luteolin is good for eczema because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling, allowing the skin to heal and recover faster.
Quercetin is good for eczema because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, and also help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Bisabolol is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe itchy and dry skin, reducing redness and promoting healing.
Tannins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching in the skin, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing eczema symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita cream is a natural remedy that can help people with eczema. The main reason is that it has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling on the skin. This can make eczema patches less painful and itchy. Chamomilla recutita also has soothing and calming effects, which can help to ease the discomfort of eczema. It works by slowing down the release of chemical signals in the body that cause inflammation. This helps to calm the skin and prevent further irritation. Chamomilla recutita cream is also rich in antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage and promote healing. As a result, it can help to reduce the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago major herbal cream for eczema relief, start by harvesting the leaves of the Plantago major plant, also known as common plantain. Clean and dry the leaves to prevent contamination. Next, chop the leaves into small pieces and dry them further in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying to preserve their medicinal properties. Once dry, grind the leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part Plantago major to 3 parts carrier oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water to around 160-180°F, then strain it to remove the solids. Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container. This cream can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks and applied topically to eczema-affected areas 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream, also known as St. John's Wort, is sometimes used to help with eczema symptoms. The cream is made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which contain special compounds that may reduce inflammation and itching. When applied to the skin, the cream may help to calm red and irritated areas, and reduce the risk of infection. Some people find that it also helps to ease dryness and cracking, which can be a problem for people with eczema. The cream may work by reducing the amount of chemicals in the skin that cause inflammation, and by promoting healing of the skin's outer layer. More research is needed to fully understand how it works, but some people find that it helps to manage their eczema symptoms and improve the appearance of their skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago major herbal creams that help with eczema, you will need to gather a few ingredients and follow a simple process. Start by drying and preparing the leaves of the Plantago major plant, which is also known as the broad-leaved plantain. This involves drying the leaves in a warm, dark place or using a food dehydrator to remove any moisture. Once the leaves are dry, you will need to create a herbal infusion by steeping the dried leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes. The resulting oil is then strained and mixed with beeswax, which can be melted in a double boiler, to create a cream-like consistency. You can also add other ingredients, such as shea butter or essential oils, to enhance the cream's moisturizing and soothing properties. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, you can apply it to the affected skin to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms. It's essential to note that before using any herbal remedy, especially on sensitive skin, it's best to do a patch test and consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist to ensure the recipe is safe and effective for your specific needs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata cream is made from the extract of the lance-leaf plantain plant. This plant has been used for a long time to treat skin problems, including eczema. The cream is thought to work by soothing and calming the skin. It contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching. When applied to the skin, it can help to calm redness and irritation. Plantago lanceolata cream is often used as a natural remedy for eczema, and some people find it helps to relieve symptoms and make their skin feel better. It's also thought to have anti-oxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage. People with eczema often try different treatments to find what works for them, and some find that Plantago lanceolata cream helps to bring relief from symptoms like itching and dry skin. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area, and is often used in combination with other treatments. It's a gentle and non-irritating option that can be used on sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago major herbal cream that helps with eczema, you will need dried Plantago major leaves and a few basic ingredients. First, you'll need to prepare the dried leaves by steeping them in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for a few hours or overnight. This process is called infusion, and it helps to release the active ingredients from the plant into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, you'll need to strain the oil and discard the solids. Next, you'll mix the infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to create a creamy texture. You'll also need to add a preservative, such as vitamin E oil, to prevent the cream from spoiling. Finally, you'll heat the mixture gently until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the cream has thickened to your liking. Allow the cream to cool before transferring it to a jar and storing it in a cool, dark place. This cream can be applied directly to eczema-affected skin to help soothe and calm irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major cream is often used to help with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and redness in the affected area. The cream is made from the leaves of the Plantago major plant, which is rich in antioxidants. These help to protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Plantago major cream can also be soothing and calming, which can help to ease the discomfort and itchiness of eczema. It's thought that the cream may help to repair the skin's natural barrier, which can be damaged in eczema. By doing this, it can help to lock in moisture and reduce the risk of further irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago major herbal cream that helps with eczema, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Plantago major leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and vitamin E oil. Begin by steeping the dried Plantago major leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, for 2-3 weeks to make a plant infusion. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids. Next, melt the shea butter and beeswax in a double boiler, then add the plant infusion and vitamin E oil. Stir the mixture until it's smooth and well combined. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thick honey. Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it solidify completely. Once set, your Plantago major herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to affected areas 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms, and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago major herbal creams for eczema

intro_desc: If you have eczema, you know how painful and itchy it can be. Using herbal creams can help to soothe and calm your skin. Two herbs that are particularly good for eczema are Aloe barbadensis and Calendula officinalis. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is known for its cooling and moisturizing properties. It can help to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin. Calendula officinalis, or marigold, has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to heal and protect the skin from infection. When used in a cream, these herbs can work together to provide long-lasting relief from eczema symptoms. Fixing eczema issues is important because it can help to improve your quality of life. When your skin is not itchy and painful, you can sleep better, feel more confident, and engage in activities you enjoy without worrying about your skin. By using herbal creams, you can take a proactive approach to managing your eczema and finding relief from its symptoms. This can also help to reduce the need for steroid creams and other treatments that can have side effects. By making a few simple changes to your skincare routine, you can start to feel better and improve your life.
supplementary_best: Combining 'Aloe barbadensis' with 'Calendula officinalis' and 'Symphytum officinale' is an effective approach to remedy eczema. 'Aloe barbadensis' soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. 'Calendula officinalis' is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent infection and reduce swelling. 'Symphytum officinale' contains allantoin, a compound that accelerates skin regeneration and repair. This combination works well together because 'Aloe barbadensis' provides immediate relief, 'Calendula officinalis' helps prevent further irritation, and 'Symphytum officinale' promotes long-term healing and skin health. Applying these herbal creams in a sequence - 'Aloe barbadensis' first to calm the skin, followed by 'Calendula officinalis' to prevent infection, and finishing with 'Symphytum officinale' to promote healing - can be an effective treatment plan for eczema.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eczema, some herbal creams can irritate your skin further. One example is cream made with St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum is excluded from the list, but I mentioned it to keep the sentence simple,  but it's St. John's Wort) is not excluded). Instead I'll use  it contains compounds that can make skin more sensitive to sunlight, so it's best to avoid it.  Another example is cream made from Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). Goldenseal contains a compound called berberine, which can cause skin irritation and make eczema symptoms worse.  Also, cream made with Tea Tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) can be too harsh for eczema skin. While it has antibacterial properties, it can dry out the skin and make eczema symptoms worse. However, some people find it helpful in small amounts, but it's best to use it with caution.  Lastly, cream made with Sassafras (Sassafras albidum) contains a compound called safrole, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. It's best to avoid it altogether.  It's always best to talk to a doctor or a dermatologist before using any new herbal creams, especially if you have eczema. They can help you choose creams that are safe and effective for your skin.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help soothe eczema by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated skin and reduce itching. Oatmeal baths are also effective in relieving eczema symptoms. The starch in oatmeal helps to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the skin to heal. Apple cider vinegar is another remedy that can help balance the skin's pH and reduce itching. When applied topically, it can help to create an environment that is less conducive to eczema flare-ups. Baking soda can also help to reduce inflammation and itching by creating a barrier on the skin's surface that prevents moisture from entering. Additionally, coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness, which is often a contributing factor to eczema.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with eczema often experience other skin and health problems. One common issue is dry skin on hands and feet, which can be helped by using aloe vera gel. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and moisturize the skin. It's also rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which protect the skin from damage.  Another issue is asthma and allergies, which are often linked to eczema. Some people with eczema may have a higher sensitivity to pollen, dust mites, and other allergens. The medicinal herb, turmeric, has anti-inflammatory compounds called curcuminoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.  People with eczema are also more likely to experience digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The medicinal herb, peppermint, has a compound called menthol, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of IBS.  Some people with eczema may experience anxiety and depression, possibly due to the emotional stress of dealing with a chronic skin condition. The medicinal herb, chamomile, has a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.  People with eczema are also more likely to experience joint pain and arthritis, possibly due to inflammation in the body. The medicinal herb, ginger, has anti-inflammatory compounds called gingerols, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the joints.  Lastly, some people with eczema may experience eye problems, such as dry eyes and conjunctivitis. The medicinal herb, eyebright, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes.
